Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that all system file permissions, ownership, and group membership on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system match the vendor-provided RPM package values. The scan executed 'rpm -Va' to compare installed file attributes against the RPM database and filtered results for permission (M), user ownership (U), and group ownership (G) deviations. No deviations were found, confirming that system files and commands have not been modified from their original vendor-supplied state. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-010010) that file permissions, ownership, and group membership of system files and commands must match vendor values. The XCP-ng Dom0 system maintains file integrity consistent with the CentOS 7-based distribution packages. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check identified system files whose permissions, ownership, or group membership differ from vendor-provided RPM package values on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'rpm -Va' and found deviations in the M (permissions), U (user), or G (group) attributes compared to the RPM database. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010010, all system file attributes must match vendor values to prevent unauthorized modification of system binaries and configuration files. Remediation: (1) Identify the package for each affected file: rpm -qf [filename]. (2) Reset user and group ownership: rpm --setugids [packagename]. (3) Reset permissions: rpm --setperms [packagename]. (4) If changes were intentional and documented, obtain ISSO/ISSM approval and document the deviation in the POA&M. The system administrator must investigate each mismatch to determine if unauthorized modification occurred.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system does not allow accounts with blank or null passwords to authenticate. The scan checked both /etc/pam.d/system-auth and /etc/pam.d/password-auth for the 'nullok' option in PAM authentication configuration. No instances of 'nullok' were found in either file, confirming that PAM is configured to reject authentication attempts from accounts with empty passwords. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-010290) that prevents logon with accounts configured with blank or null passwords. The XCP-ng Dom0 PAM configuration enforces password-based authentication for all local accounts. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system allows accounts with blank or null passwords to authenticate. The scan detected the 'nullok' option in /etc/pam.d/system-auth and/or /etc/pam.d/password-auth, which permits authentication without a password for accounts that have no password set. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010290, the operating system must not allow accounts configured with blank or null passwords. This represents a CAT I (high severity) finding because it could allow unauthorized access to the system. Remediation: (1) Remove all instances of 'nullok' from /etc/pam.d/system-auth. (2) Remove all instances of 'nullok' from /etc/pam.d/password-auth. (3) Verify no accounts exist with empty passwords: awk -F: '($2 == "") {print}' /etc/shadow. (4) Set passwords for any accounts found without one. The system administrator must ensure all accounts have valid passwords.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the SSH daemon on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system does not allow authentication using empty passwords. The scan checked /etc/ssh/sshd_config for the PermitEmptyPasswords directive. The setting was confirmed as 'no' (or defaulting to 'no' when not explicitly set), which prevents SSH clients from authenticating with accounts that have no password configured. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-010300) that the SSH daemon must not allow authentication using an empty password. SSH is the primary remote access method for XCP-ng Dom0 administration, making this control critical for preventing unauthorized remote access. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the SSH daemon on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system may allow authentication using empty passwords. The scan checked /etc/ssh/sshd_config and found that PermitEmptyPasswords is not set to 'no'. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010300, the SSH daemon must not allow authentication using empty passwords. This is a CAT I finding because SSH is the primary remote administration interface for XCP-ng Dom0, and permitting empty password authentication could allow unauthorized access to the hypervisor. Remediation: (1) Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config and add or modify: PermitEmptyPasswords no. (2) Restart the SSH service: systemctl restart sshd. (3) Verify the change: grep -i PermitEmptyPasswords /etc/ssh/sshd_config. (4) Ensure no accounts exist with empty passwords. The system administrator must apply this change immediately given the severity.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system does not allow unattended or automatic logon via the graphical user interface. The scan checked /etc/gdm/custom.conf for the AutomaticLoginEnable parameter. The setting was confirmed as 'false' or not present (defaults to disabled), preventing automatic graphical logons without authentication. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-010440) that unattended or automatic logon must be disabled. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system allows unattended or automatic logon via the graphical user interface. The AutomaticLoginEnable parameter in /etc/gdm/custom.conf is not set to 'false'. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010440, the operating system must not allow automatic logon to prevent unauthorized access. Remediation: (1) Edit /etc/gdm/custom.conf. (2) In the [daemon] section, set: AutomaticLoginEnable=false. (3) Restart the display manager or reboot. The system administrator must ensure no automatic logon is configured for the graphical interface.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: GNOME desktop environment is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 system. XCP-ng Dom0 is a server-oriented hypervisor management domain that typically operates without a graphical user interface. Since the graphical user interface requirement does not apply to this system configuration, per the STIG guidance ('if the system does not have GNOME installed, this requirement is Not Applicable'), this check is Not Applicable.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system does not allow unrestricted (timed) logon via the graphical user interface. The scan checked /etc/gdm/custom.conf for the TimedLoginEnable parameter. The setting was confirmed as 'false' or not present (defaults to disabled), preventing timed automatic logons without authentication. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-010450). Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system allows unrestricted timed logon via the graphical user interface. The TimedLoginEnable parameter in /etc/gdm/custom.conf is not set to 'false'. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010450, the operating system must not allow unrestricted logon. Remediation: (1) Edit /etc/gdm/custom.conf. (2) In the [daemon] section, set: TimedLoginEnable=false. (3) Restart the display manager or reboot. The system administrator must disable timed logon immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: GNOME desktop environment is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 system. XCP-ng Dom0 is a server-oriented hypervisor management domain that typically operates without a graphical user interface. Since the graphical user interface requirement does not apply, per the STIG guidance ('if the system does not have GNOME installed, this requirement is Not Applicable'), this check is Not Applicable.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system with BIOS firmware requires GRUB2 authentication for single-user and maintenance modes. The scan confirmed that /boot/grub2/user.cfg contains a GRUB2_PASSWORD entry using grub.pbkdf2.sha512 encryption. This ensures that unauthorized users cannot boot into single-user mode to bypass authentication controls. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-010482) for BIOS-based systems. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system with BIOS firmware does not have a GRUB2 superuser password configured or the password is not using PBKDF2-SHA512 encryption. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010482, BIOS systems must require authentication to boot into single-user and maintenance modes. Without boot authentication, an attacker with physical or console access could boot into single-user mode and gain root access without credentials. Remediation: (1) Run 'grub2-setpassword' to set an encrypted GRUB2 superuser password. (2) Verify the password is set: grep -iw grub2_password /boot/grub2/user.cfg. The system administrator must configure boot authentication immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: This system uses UEFI firmware, not BIOS. Per the STIG guidance ('For systems that use UEFI, this is Not Applicable'), this BIOS-specific GRUB2 authentication check does not apply. The corresponding UEFI check (V-204440) applies instead.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system with UEFI firmware requires GRUB2 authentication for single-user and maintenance modes. The scan confirmed that the EFI GRUB2 user.cfg contains a GRUB2_PASSWORD entry using grub.pbkdf2.sha512 encryption. This ensures unauthorized users cannot boot into single-user mode to bypass authentication. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010491 for UEFI systems. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system with UEFI firmware does not have a GRUB2 superuser password configured in the EFI partition. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010491, UEFI systems must require authentication to boot into single-user and maintenance modes. Remediation: (1) Run 'grub2-setpassword' to set an encrypted GRUB2 superuser password. (2) Verify: grep -iw grub2_password /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/user.cfg (or centos path). The system administrator must configure UEFI boot authentication immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: This system uses BIOS firmware, not UEFI. Per the STIG guidance ('For systems that use BIOS, this is Not Applicable'), this UEFI-specific GRUB2 authentication check does not apply. The corresponding BIOS check (V-204438) applies instead.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the rsh-server package is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'rpm -q rsh-server' and confirmed the package is not present. The rsh (Remote Shell) service provides unencrypted remote access that does not protect the confidentiality or integrity of transmitted data. Its absence confirms compliance with RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020000. XCP-ng Dom0 uses SSH for all remote administration, which provides encrypted communications. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the rsh-server package is installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020000, the rsh-server package must not be installed because it provides unencrypted remote access that does not protect confidentiality or integrity of user credentials and session data. This is a CAT I finding. Remediation: (1) Remove the package immediately: yum remove rsh-server. (2) Verify removal: rpm -q rsh-server. (3) Ensure SSH is configured as the sole remote access method. The system administrator must remove this package and ensure only encrypted remote access protocols are available.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the ypserv (NIS server) package is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'rpm -q ypserv' and confirmed the package is not present. NIS (Network Information Service) provides an unencrypted authentication service that does not protect the confidentiality or integrity of user passwords or remote sessions. Its absence confirms compliance with RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020010. XCP-ng Dom0 uses modern authentication methods that provide encrypted credential handling. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the ypserv (NIS server) package is installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020010, the ypserv package must not be installed because NIS provides unencrypted authentication that exposes user passwords and session data. This is a CAT I finding. Remediation: (1) Remove the package immediately: yum remove ypserv. (2) Verify removal: rpm -q ypserv. (3) Migrate any NIS-dependent authentication to LDAP/Kerberos or local accounts. The system administrator must remove this package and transition to encrypted authentication services.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that yum on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system is configured to verify GPG signatures for repository packages. The scan confirmed that gpgcheck=1 is set in /etc/yum.conf. This ensures that all packages installed from repositories have been digitally signed by a trusted Certificate Authority, preventing installation of tampered or unauthorized software. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020050 for repository package signature verification. XCP-ng repositories use GPG-signed packages to maintain supply chain integrity. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that yum on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system is not configured to verify GPG signatures for repository packages. The gpgcheck setting in /etc/yum.conf is not set to '1'. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020050, the operating system must verify digital signatures before installing any packages from repositories. Without GPG verification, tampered or malicious packages could be installed. Remediation: (1) Edit /etc/yum.conf and set: gpgcheck=1 in the [main] section. (2) Verify all repository configuration files in /etc/yum.repos.d/ also have gpgcheck=1. (3) Ensure GPG keys for XCP-ng repositories are imported. The system administrator must enable signature verification immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that yum on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system is configured to verify GPG signatures for local packages. The scan confirmed that localpkg_gpgcheck=1 is set in /etc/yum.conf. This ensures that locally-installed RPM packages must also be digitally signed by a trusted Certificate Authority, preventing installation of tampered or unauthorized local packages. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020060 for local package signature verification. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that yum on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system is not configured to verify GPG signatures for local packages. The localpkg_gpgcheck setting in /etc/yum.conf is not set to '1'. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020060, the operating system must verify digital signatures before installing local packages. Without this check, manually-downloaded RPM files could be installed without signature verification. Remediation: (1) Edit /etc/yum.conf and set: localpkg_gpgcheck=1 in the [main] section. (2) Verify the change: grep localpkg_gpgcheck /etc/yum.conf. The system administrator must enable local package signature verification.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the Ctrl-Alt-Delete key sequence is disabled on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan confirmed that the ctrl-alt-del.target systemd unit is masked, preventing the key combination from triggering an unintended system reboot. A masked unit cannot be started or enabled by any means, providing the strongest form of disablement. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020230 for disabling the Ctrl-Alt-Delete reboot sequence. On a hypervisor Dom0 system, preventing accidental reboots is critical to maintaining virtual machine availability. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the Ctrl-Alt-Delete key sequence is not properly disabled on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The ctrl-alt-del.target systemd unit is not masked, which means the key combination could trigger an unintended system reboot. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020230, the Ctrl-Alt-Delete sequence must be disabled to prevent accidental or malicious reboots. On a hypervisor Dom0, an unintended reboot would shut down all running virtual machines. Remediation: (1) Disable the target: systemctl disable ctrl-alt-del.target. (2) Mask the target: systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target. (3) Verify: systemctl status ctrl-alt-del.target (should show 'masked'). The system administrator must apply this change immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the Ctrl-Alt-Delete key sequence is disabled in the graphical user interface on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan confirmed that the dconf setting for the GNOME logout action is configured to prevent Ctrl-Alt-Delete from triggering a logout/reboot in the GUI session. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020231. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the Ctrl-Alt-Delete key sequence is not disabled in the graphical user interface on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The dconf logout setting is not configured to prevent Ctrl-Alt-Delete from triggering a GUI logout/reboot. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020231, the key sequence must be disabled in the GUI. Remediation: (1) Create /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-disable-CAD. (2) Add: [org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys] and logout=''. (3) Run: dconf update. The system administrator must apply this change.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: GNOME desktop environment is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 system. XCP-ng Dom0 is a server-oriented hypervisor management domain that typically operates without a graphical user interface. Per the STIG guidance ('if the operating system does not have a graphical user interface installed, this requirement is Not Applicable'), this check is Not Applicable.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that this XCP-ng Dom0 system is running a vendor-supported operating system release. While XCP-ng Dom0 is based on CentOS 7 (RHEL 7), it receives active security updates and maintenance from Vates, the XCP-ng vendor. The scan confirmed the XCP-ng version is 8.x, which is within the current Vates support lifecycle. XCP-ng maintains its own package repositories with security patches independent of RHEL 7 end-of-life status. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-020250) that the operating system must be a vendor-supported release, as Vates provides ongoing vendor support for the XCP-ng platform. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check could not confirm that this XCP-ng Dom0 system is running a vendor-supported release. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020250, the operating system must be a vendor-supported release to ensure security patches and updates are available. While XCP-ng is based on CentOS 7/RHEL 7, it must be within the Vates support lifecycle to receive security updates. Remediation: (1) Verify the XCP-ng version: cat /etc/xcp-ng-release. (2) Check Vates support lifecycle for your version at the XCP-ng project website. (3) If the version is end-of-life, plan migration to a supported version. (4) Ensure XCP-ng repositories are configured for receiving security updates. The system administrator must maintain the system within vendor support.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that only the root account has UID 0 on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'awk' against /etc/passwd to identify all accounts with UID 0 and confirmed that root is the only account with unrestricted (UID 0) access. No duplicate UID 0 accounts were found. This meets the RHEL 7 STIG requirement (RHEL-07-020310) that root must be the only account with UID 0, preventing unauthorized accounts from having unrestricted system access on the hypervisor management domain. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that accounts other than root have UID 0 on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-020310, only the root account may have UID 0. Additional UID 0 accounts have unrestricted access to the entire system, which on a hypervisor Dom0 means full control over all virtual machines. Remediation: (1) Identify the non-root UID 0 accounts: awk -F: '$3 == 0 {print $1}' /etc/passwd. (2) Change each account's UID: usermod -u [new_uid] [username]. (3) System accounts should use UID 1-999, regular accounts 1000+. (4) Verify file ownership after UID changes. The system administrator must remove all duplicate UID 0 accounts immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system implements FIPS-validated cryptography. The scan confirmed that the dracut-fips package is installed, the fips=1 kernel parameter is set in the boot command line, and the runtime FIPS mode is active (/proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled = 1). This ensures all cryptographic operations use NIST FIPS 140-2 validated algorithms for digital signatures, cryptographic hashing, and data-at-rest protection as required by RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-021350. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system does not fully implement FIPS-validated cryptography. The scan detected that either the dracut-fips package is not installed, the fips=1 kernel parameter is missing from the boot command line, or FIPS mode is not active at runtime. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-021350, the operating system must implement NIST FIPS-validated cryptography. Note: XCP-ng Dom0 may not support FIPS mode due to its custom Xen kernel. This is a known compliance gap requiring Vates vendor resolution. Remediation: (1) Install dracut-fips: yum install dracut-fips. (2) Add fips=1 to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX in /etc/default/grub. (3) Regenerate GRUB config: grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg. (4) Rebuild initramfs: dracut -f. (5) Reboot. Contact Vates for XCP-ng FIPS compatibility guidance.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the telnet-server package is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'rpm -q telnet-server' and confirmed the package is not present. Telnet provides an unencrypted remote access service that transmits credentials and session data in cleartext, making it unsuitable for DoD environments. Its absence confirms compliance with RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-021710. XCP-ng Dom0 uses SSH for all remote administration. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the telnet-server package is installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-021710, telnet-server must not be installed because it transmits all data including passwords in cleartext. This is a CAT I finding on a hypervisor management domain. Remediation: (1) Remove immediately: yum remove telnet-server. (2) Verify removal: rpm -q telnet-server. (3) Ensure SSH is the sole remote access method. The system administrator must remove this package immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the SSH daemon on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system is configured to use only the SSHv2 protocol. The scan checked /etc/ssh/sshd_config for the Protocol directive and confirmed that SSHv1 is not enabled. XCP-ng is based on CentOS 7.5+, which defaults to SSHv2 only. SSHv1 has known cryptographic weaknesses and must not be used. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040390. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the SSH daemon on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system may allow the SSHv1 protocol. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040390, only SSHv2 is permitted. SSHv1 has critical cryptographic vulnerabilities. Remediation: (1) Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config. (2) Set: Protocol 2. (3) Remove any lines referencing Protocol 1. (4) Restart sshd: systemctl restart sshd. The system administrator must disable SSHv1 immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that no .shosts files exist on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan searched the entire filesystem (with depth limits) for files matching '*.shosts' and found none. The .shosts file provides host-based authentication for rsh-related services, which use unencrypted communications. Their absence confirms compliance with RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040540. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found .shosts files on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040540, no .shosts files may exist on the system. These files enable host-based authentication for rsh services which transmit data unencrypted. Remediation: Remove all .shosts files found: rm [path]/.shosts. Investigate why they were created and ensure rsh-based services are not in use. The system administrator must remove these files immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that no shosts.equiv files exist on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan searched the entire filesystem (with depth limits) for files named 'shosts.equiv' and found none. The shosts.equiv file provides system-wide host-based authentication for rsh services, which use unencrypted communications. Their absence confirms compliance with RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040550. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found shosts.equiv files on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040550, no shosts.equiv files may exist. These files enable system-wide host-based authentication for rsh services which transmit data unencrypted. Remediation: Remove all shosts.equiv files: rm [path]/shosts.equiv. Investigate why they were created. The system administrator must remove these files immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the vsftpd (FTP server) package is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'rpm -q vsftpd' and confirmed the package is not present. FTP transmits data and credentials in cleartext and must not be installed on DoD systems unless documented as an operational requirement with the ISSO. Its absence confirms compliance with RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040690. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the vsftpd (FTP server) package is installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040690, FTP server must not be installed unless documented with the ISSO as an operational requirement. FTP transmits credentials in cleartext. Remediation: (1) If not required: yum remove vsftpd. (2) If operationally required: document the requirement with the ISSO and implement compensating controls (SFTP/FTPS). The system administrator must address this finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that the tftp-server package is not installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'rpm -q tftp-server' and confirmed the package is not present. TFTP provides an unencrypted, unauthenticated file transfer service and must not be installed unless required for specific operational support (e.g., PXE boot). Its absence confirms compliance with RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040700. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found that the tftp-server package is installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040700, TFTP server must not be installed unless documented with the ISSO as required for operational support. TFTP has no authentication mechanism. Remediation: (1) If not required: yum remove tftp-server. (2) If required for PXE: document with the ISSO and restrict access via firewall rules. The system administrator must address this finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that SNMP community strings on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system are not set to default values. The scan checked /etc/snmp/snmpd.conf for the default 'public' and 'private' community strings and found neither in active (non-commented) configuration lines. Custom community strings prevent unauthorized SNMP access to system information. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040800. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found default SNMP community strings on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The 'public' and/or 'private' community strings were detected in /etc/snmp/snmpd.conf. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-040800, default community strings must be changed to prevent unauthorized SNMP reconnaissance. Remediation: (1) Edit /etc/snmp/snmpd.conf. (2) Replace all 'public' and 'private' community strings with unique organizational values. (3) Restart snmpd: systemctl restart snmpd. Consider using SNMPv3 with authentication and encryption. The system administrator must change default community strings.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: SNMP is not configured on this XCP-ng Dom0 system. The /etc/snmp/snmpd.conf configuration file does not exist, indicating SNMP service is not installed or configured. Per the STIG guidance ('If the file does not exist, this is Not Applicable'), this check does not apply to this system.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that cryptographic hashes of system files and commands on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system match vendor-provided RPM package values. The scan executed 'rpm -Va --noconfig' and filtered for MD5 hash mismatches (flag position 3 = '5'). Configuration files were excluded as they are expected to change. No hash mismatches were found for system binaries or libraries, confirming file integrity is maintained. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010020. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found system files whose cryptographic hashes differ from vendor RPM package values on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan executed 'rpm -Va --noconfig' and detected MD5 hash mismatches for non-configuration files. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010020, system file hashes must match vendor values to ensure integrity. Hash changes may indicate unauthorized modification, compromise, or corruption. Remediation: (1) Identify the package for each file: rpm -qf [filename]. (2) Reinstall affected packages: yum reinstall [package]. (3) Investigate whether changes were authorized. (4) Review system audit logs for evidence of tampering. The system administrator must investigate and remediate all hash mismatches.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that an antivirus solution is installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan checked for common antivirus packages including ClamAV, McAfee, and CrowdStrike, and confirmed that at least one endpoint protection solution is present. Antivirus software is required on all DoD systems to detect and prevent malware. This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-032000 for virus scan program installation on the hypervisor management domain. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found no antivirus solution installed on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. The scan checked for ClamAV, McAfee, and CrowdStrike and found none. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-032000, all systems must have an antivirus program installed. The hypervisor management domain (Dom0) has access to all virtual machine resources and must be protected against malware. Remediation: (1) Install an approved antivirus: yum install clamav clamav-update (for ClamAV). (2) Configure automatic signature updates. (3) Enable on-access scanning if supported. (4) Schedule regular full system scans. The ISSO must ensure an approved antivirus solution is deployed on all XCP-ng hosts.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check verified that no accounts on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system have blank or null passwords in /etc/shadow. The scan used awk to check for empty password fields in the shadow file and found none. All accounts either have a valid password hash or are locked (indicated by '!' or '*' in the password field). This meets RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010291 that prevents accounts with blank passwords from existing on the system. Finding: Not a Finding.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.

Answer.ValidTrueComment: The automated check found accounts with blank or null passwords in /etc/shadow on this XCP-ng Dom0 (RHEL 7-based) system. Per RHEL 7 STIG requirement RHEL-07-010291, no accounts may have blank passwords as this allows authentication without credentials. This is a CAT I finding on a hypervisor management domain. Remediation: For each affected account: (1) Set a password: passwd [username]. (2) Or lock the account: passwd -l [username]. (3) Verify no blank passwords remain: awk -F: '!$2 {print $1}' /etc/shadow. The system administrator must remediate all blank password accounts immediately.
Answer.ValidFalseComment: This answer index should not normally be used.
